description_long: This chapel features a rectangular stone structure with four tall, greenish spires at each corner, each topped with a cross. The front facade is adorned with three pointed-arch windows, a central circular window with a clover motif, and a single wooden door. The roof is steeply pitched with three prominent gables, each capped with green trim and a cross at the central peak. The side and rear walls are simpler, with two pointed-arch windows on each side and a shingled roof pattern. The building's exterior is primarily gray stone with green accents, giving it a Gothic Revival appearance. The overall design is symmetrical and formal, with religious iconography subtly integrated into the architecture.
description: A Gothic Revival-style stone chapel with four tall spires, pointed-arch windows, and a steeply pitched roof with green trim. The front features a circular window with a clover motif and a single wooden door.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with a wooden door, three pointed-arch windows, a circular clover-motif window, and all four spires with crosses.
description_view_1: The rear view displays two pointed-arch windows and the backs of the spires, with a clear view of the shingled roof and no entrance.
description_view_2: The side view highlights the steep gabled roof, the green trim, and the symmetrical arrangement of the spires, with no visible doors or windows on this side.
description_view_3: The opposite rear view mirrors view 1, showing two pointed-arch windows and the shingled roof, with the spires visible at the corners.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is rectangular, made of dark brown wood, and set within a pointed-arch stone frame. It is simple in design, lacking ornate carvings or metalwork, and complements the Gothic style of the chapel.
